content: Maternal mortality assessment includes: (1) Case identification—death certificate ICD-10 codes (O00-O99 pregnancy-related), linkage with birth certificates, pregnancy checkbox improvement; (2) Classification—pregnancy-associated (during or within year of pregnancy), pregnancy-related (caused by pregnancy complications), preventable (could be avoided with different care); (3) Surveillance systems—CDC Pregnancy Mortality Surveillance System, state Maternal Mortality Review Committees, WHO Maternal Death Surveillance and Response; (4) Near-miss monitoring—severe maternal morbidity indicators (blood transfusion, hysterectomy, ICU admission, cardiac arrest) as quality metrics; (5) Risk stratification—preconception risk assessment, social determinants screening, comorbidity indices. Limitations include underreporting, misclassification, varying definitions across jurisdictions, and small numbers limiting statistical analysis. Real-time surveillance emerging through electronic health records.
